---
title: "5_260302_Robustness"
author: "Arturo Bertero"
date: "2026-03-02"
output: html_document
---

# Input

```{r}
# Libraries
library(pacman)
p_load(tidyverse, here, mgm, bootnet, reshape2, ggplot2, huge, conflicted,
       qgraph, patchwork, doParallel, foreach)

# Options
rm(list = ls())
options(scipen = 999)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter, dplyr::select)

# Data
master_datasets <- readRDS(here("Input", "Secondary", "master_datasets.rds"))
```

# Processing

## Preparation

```{r}
H1_matrices_npn <- list()
H2_matrices_npn <- list()
Full_items_npn  <- list() 

for (study_name in names(master_datasets)) {
  df <- master_datasets[[study_name]]
  
  df_items <- df %>% select(country, starts_with("cm_"), starts_with("cb_")) %>% na.omit()
  df_indices <- df %>% select(country, CM, CB) %>% na.omit() 
  
  countries <- unique(df_items$country)
  
  for (cn in countries) {
    list_name <- paste0(study_name, "_", cn)
    
    # Transform ALL items once
    raw_items <- as.matrix(df_items[df_items$country == cn, -1])
    npn_items  <- huge.npn(raw_items, verbose = FALSE)
    Full_items_npn[[list_name]] <- npn_items
    
    # Transform Indices
    raw_indices <- as.matrix(df_indices[df_indices$country == cn, c("CM", "CB")])
    npn_indices <- huge.npn(raw_indices, verbose = FALSE)
    
    # Build H1/H2 lists
    H1_matrices_npn[[list_name]] <- cbind(CM = npn_indices[,"CM"], 
                                         npn_items[, grep("^cb_", colnames(npn_items))])
    
    H2_matrices_npn[[list_name]] <- cbind(CB = npn_indices[,"CB"], 
                                         npn_items[, grep("^cm_", colnames(npn_items))])
  }
}
```

## Montecarlo simulation

```{r}
# Register the 16 cores
cl <- makeCluster(12)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Ensure the seed is handled correctly for parallel processes
set.seed(123)
B <- 1000
monte_carlo_results <- list()

# ==========================================
# 2. Parallelized Loop
# ==========================================
for (name in names(Full_items_npn)) {
    
    data_matrix_npn <- Full_items_npn[[name]] 
    all_cm <- grep("^cm_", colnames(data_matrix_npn), value = TRUE)
    all_cb <- grep("^cb_", colnames(data_matrix_npn), value = TRUE)
    k <- min(length(all_cm), length(all_cb))
    
    cat("Parallelizing Monte Carlo for:", name, "...\n")
    
    # foreach will run B times and combine results into a single dataframe
    # .packages ensures each core knows what 'mgm' is
    dataset_results <- foreach(i = 1:B, .combine = 'rbind', .packages = 'mgm') %dopar% {
      
      # Resample items
      sampled_cm <- if(length(all_cm) > k) sample(all_cm, k) else all_cm
      sampled_cb <- if(length(all_cb) > k) sample(all_cb, k) else all_cb
      
      iter_matrix <- data_matrix_npn[, c(sampled_cm, sampled_cb)]
      
      # Estimate MGM
      mgm_iter <- mgm::mgm(iter_matrix, type = rep("g", 2*k), level = rep(1, 2*k),
                           lambdaSel = "EBIC", lambdaGam = 0.25, ruleReg = "OR", 
                           verbose = FALSE, pbar = FALSE)
      
      # Calculate Strength & Fix Names
      strengths <- colSums(abs(mgm_iter$pairwise$wadj))
      names(strengths) <- colnames(iter_matrix)
      
      # 1. Record Hub Winner
      winners <- names(strengths)[strengths == max(strengths)]
      hub_win <- as.numeric(any(winners %in% sampled_cm))
      
      # 2. Record Delta Mean
      mean_cm <- mean(strengths[sampled_cm])
      mean_cb <- mean(strengths[sampled_cb])
      delta_m <- mean_cb - mean_cm
      
      # Return results for this single iteration
      return(data.frame(CM_Top1 = hub_win, Delta_Mean = delta_m))
    }
    
    # Add the dataset name to the resulting dataframe and store in list
    dataset_results$Dataset <- name
    monte_carlo_results[[name]] <- dataset_results
}

# ==========================================
# 3. Cleanup & Save
# ==========================================
stopCluster(cl) # Very important: shut down the workers!

saveRDS(monte_carlo_results, here("Input", "Secondary", "monte_carlo_results.rds"))

#Load to save time
# monte_carlo_results <- readRDS(here("Input", "Secondary", "monte_carlo_results.rds"))
```


### Plots

#### Prop of times any cm item is the most central node

```{r}
mc_data_clean <- do.call(rbind, monte_carlo_results) %>%
  mutate(
    CountryRaw = str_remove_all(Dataset, "Study1_SUSPECTS_|Study2_ACTS_|Study3_CMQ_|Study3_GCB_"),
    CountryName = case_when(
      CountryRaw == "DEU" ~ "Germany", CountryRaw == "ESP" ~ "Spain",
      CountryRaw == "FRA" ~ "France", CountryRaw == "GBR" ~ "United Kingdom",
      CountryRaw == "ITA" ~ "Italy", CountryRaw == "POL" ~ "Poland",
      CountryRaw == "SWE" ~ "Sweden", CountryRaw %in% c("USA", "USA_ACTS") ~ "United States",
      TRUE ~ CountryRaw 
    ),
    Scale = case_when(
      grepl("Study1", Dataset) ~ "GCB5", grepl("Study2", Dataset) ~ "ACTS",
      grepl("Study3_CMQ", Dataset) ~ "CMQ", grepl("Study3_GCB", Dataset) ~ "GCB"
    ),
    FacetTitle = paste0(CountryName, " (", Scale, ")")
  )
```

```{r}
#Define a global theme
theme_masterpiece <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11, base_family = "serif") +
    theme(
      # Text elements
      text = element_text(family = "serif", color = "#1A1A1A"),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15, family = "serif", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 15)),
      plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22, family = "serif"),
      
      # Facet Strip
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#EFEFEF", color = "#333333", size = 0.6),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, family = "serif", color = "#1A1A1A"),
      
      # Grid Logic
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#D1D1D1", size = 0.4, linetype = "dotted"),
      
      # Background
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "#333333", size = 0.7),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#F9F9F7", color = NA), 
      
      # Axis Styling
      axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, margin = margin(r = 12)),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8.5, color = "black", face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9.5, color = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(color = "#333333", size = 0.5),
      
      # Legend 
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
    )
}
```

```{r}
summary_data <- mc_data_clean %>%
  group_by(FacetTitle, Scale) %>%
  summarise(
    Successes = sum(CM_Top1),
    Total = n(),
    Probability = mean(CM_Top1),
    Lower_CI = binom.test(sum(CM_Top1), n())$conf.int[1],
    Upper_CI = binom.test(sum(CM_Top1), n())$conf.int[2],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = reorder(FacetTitle, Probability), y = Probability)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, color = "#D3D3D3", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Lower_CI, ymax = Upper_CI), width = 0.3, color = "#455A64", size = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Probability > 0.5), size = 4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#006B3C", "FALSE" = "#E41A1C")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_flip() + 
  labs(
    title = "Monte Carlo Robustness: Hub Status Probability",
    subtitle = "Probability that a Conspiracy Mentality item is the most central node (Equal-Item Comparison)",
    x = NULL, y = "Probability of CM being the Network Hub"
  ) +
  theme_masterpiece() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Output", "Robustness", "MC", "MC_Final_Hub_Probability.png"), width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 600, type = "cairo")
```

#### Distribution on the deltas of CM and CB items

```{r}
# 1. Prepare the data: Order facets by the median Delta_Mean for a professional flow
mc_data_ordered <- mc_data_clean %>%
  group_by(FacetTitle) %>%
  mutate(Median_Delta = median(Delta_Mean)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(FacetTitle = reorder(FacetTitle, Median_Delta))

# 2. Build the Masterpiece Violin Plot
ggplot(mc_data_ordered, aes(x = "Simulations", y = Delta_Mean)) +
  # The Zero Line (The Null Hypothesis reference)
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "#E41A1C", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # The Violin: Using the Deep Purple from your CB palette
  geom_violin(fill = "#702963", color = "#1A1A1A", alpha = 0.7, size = 0.6) +
  
  # The Boxplot inside for precise median/quartile tracking
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, fill = "white", color = "#1A1A1A", outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.6) +
  
  # Facet by Country/Study
  facet_wrap(~ FacetTitle, ncol = 4) + 
  
  labs(
    title = "Monte Carlo Robustness: Distribution of Centrality Differences",
    subtitle = "Delta Mean (CB Strength - CM Strength). Values > 0 indicate CB items are more central on average.",
    x = NULL, 
    y = expression(paste(Delta, " Mean (Strength Centrality)"))
  ) +
  
  # Apply your custom masterpiece theme
  theme_masterpiece() +
  theme(
    # Clean up the X-axis since "Simulations" is redundant across facets
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    
    # Ensure the Y-axis is crisp
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    
    # Formatting the facets to match your masterpiece style
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#EFEFEF", color = "#333333"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9)
  )

# 3. Save with high resolution
ggsave(here("Output", "Robustness", "MC", "MC_Final_Delta_Mean_Violin.png"), 
       width = 11, height = 8, dpi = 600, type = "cairo")
```


## Case dropping bootstrap of every network

```{r}
# Case dropping bootstrap with bootnet

# all matrices into a master list
all_matrices <- list()
for (name in names(Full_items_npn)) {
  all_matrices[[paste0("H0_", name)]] <- Full_items_npn[[name]]
  all_matrices[[paste0("H1_", name)]] <- H1_matrices_npn[[name]]
  all_matrices[[paste0("H2_", name)]] <- H2_matrices_npn[[name]]
}

# # case-dropping bootstraps
# set.seed(123)
# case_boots <- list()
# 
# for (name in names(all_matrices)) {
#   mat <- all_matrices[[name]]
#   k <- ncol(mat)
#   
#   fit <- estimateNetwork(mat, default = "mgm", 
#                          type = rep("g", k), level = rep(1, k),
#                          verbose = FALSE)
#   
#   case_boots[[name]] <- bootnet(fit, nBoots = 1000, type = "case", 
#                                 nCores = 16, statistics = "strength")
# }
#
## Save 
#saveRDS(case_boots, here("Input", "Secondary", "boot_case_master.rds"))

# or load
case_boots = readRDS(here("Input", "Secondary", "boot_case_master.rds"))
```


```{r}
# Extract data

# Initialize empty lists for the tables
centrality_tables <- list()
cs_tables <- list()

for (name in names(case_boots)) {
  boot_obj <- case_boots[[name]]
  
  # Extract centrality 
  cent_df <- centralityTable(boot_obj$sample, standardized = TRUE) %>% 
    filter(measure == "Strength") %>%
    mutate(Network = name) %>%
    select(Network, Node = node, Strength = value)
  
  centrality_tables[[name]] <- cent_df
  
  # Extract CS-Coefficient 
  cs_val <- corStability(boot_obj, cor = 0.7, statistics = "strength")
  
  cs_tables[[name]] <- data.frame(
    Network = name,
    CS_Coefficient = cs_val
  )
}

# Bind into master dataframes
master_centrality <- do.call(rbind, centrality_tables)
master_cs <- do.call(rbind, cs_tables)
```


### Tables 

```{r}
# Export 
write.csv(master_centrality, here("Output", "Robustness", "Stability", "Appendix_Centrality_Tables.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(master_cs, here("Output", "Robustness", "Stability", "Appendix_CS_Coefficients.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```


### Plots

#### Stability plots

```{r}
plot_library <- list()

for (name in names(case_boots)) {
  
  # Parse the name (e.g., "H1_Study3_CMQ_FRA")
  parts <- str_split(name, "_", n = 2)[[1]]
  hyp <- parts[1] # e.g., "H1"
  rest <- parts[2] # e.g., "Study3_CMQ_FRA"
  
  # Extract country and study 
  rest_parts <- str_split(rest, "_")[[1]]
  country_raw <- tail(rest_parts, 1)
  study <- str_remove(rest, paste0("_", country_raw, "$"))
  
  # Clean country names for the title
  country_clean <- case_when(
    country_raw == "DEU" ~ "Germany",
    country_raw == "ESP" ~ "Spain",
    country_raw == "FRA" ~ "France",
    country_raw == "GBR" ~ "United Kingdom",
    country_raw == "ITA" ~ "Italy",
    country_raw == "POL" ~ "Poland",
    country_raw == "SWE" ~ "Sweden",
    country_raw %in% c("USA", "USA_ACTS") ~ "United States",
    TRUE ~ country_raw
  )
  
  # Build the Plot
  p <- plot(case_boots[[name]], statistics = "strength") +
    labs(
      title = country_clean, 
      x = "Fraction of Cases Dropped",
      y = "Correlation"
    ) +
    theme_masterpiece() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none", 
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5) 
    )
  
  # Initialize list levels 
  if (is.null(plot_library[[study]])) plot_library[[study]] <- list()
  if (is.null(plot_library[[study]][[hyp]])) plot_library[[study]][[hyp]] <- list()
  
  # Store the plot
  plot_library[[study]][[hyp]][[country_clean]] <- p
}

# Now we assemble the final figures

for (study in names(plot_library)) {
  
  # Study 1 and 3 have same logic
  if (str_starts(study, "Study1") | str_starts(study, "Study3")) {
    
    for (hyp in names(plot_library[[study]])) {
      
      # Extract all country plots 
      p_list <- plot_library[[study]][[hyp]]
      
      # Assemble 
      combined_panel <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = 2) +
        plot_annotation(
          theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(family = "serif", face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.5))
        )
      
      # Export 
      ggsave(here("Output", "Robustness", "Stability", paste0("Panel_", study, "_", hyp, ".png")),
             plot = combined_panel, width = 8, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo")
    }
    
  # Study 2
  } else if (str_starts(study, "Study2")) {
    
    # Overwrite the default country title with the specific hypothesis
    p_h0 <- plot_library[[study]][["H0"]][[1]] + labs(title = "H0", subtitle = NULL)
    p_h1 <- plot_library[[study]][["H1"]][[1]] + labs(title = "H1", subtitle = NULL)
    p_h2 <- plot_library[[study]][["H2"]][[1]] + labs(title = "H2", subtitle = NULL)
    
    # Assemble 
    combined_s2 <- (p_h0 / p_h1 / p_h2) +
      plot_annotation(
        theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(family = "serif", face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.5))
      )
    
    # Export
    ggsave(here("Output", "Robustness", "Stability", paste0("Panel_", study, "_All_Hypotheses.png")),
           plot = combined_s2, width = 8, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo")
  }
}
```

#### Centrality plots

```{r}
# Rebuild the all_matrices list just to extract the true column names
all_matrices <- list()
for (name in names(Full_items_npn)) {
  all_matrices[[paste0("H0_", name)]] <- Full_items_npn[[name]]
  all_matrices[[paste0("H1_", name)]] <- H1_matrices_npn[[name]]
  all_matrices[[paste0("H2_", name)]] <- H2_matrices_npn[[name]]
}

# Take node names
mapping_list <- list()
for (name in names(all_matrices)) {
  mat <- all_matrices[[name]]
  mapping_list[[name]] <- data.frame(
    Network = name,
    Node_Num = as.character(1:ncol(mat)),
    Actual_Node = colnames(mat)
  )
}
node_dict <- do.call(rbind, mapping_list)

# Data cleaning
cent_plot_data <- master_centrality %>%
  mutate(Node_Num = as.character(parse_number(as.character(Node)))) %>%
  left_join(node_dict, by = c("Network", "Node_Num")) %>%
  mutate(Node = ifelse(!is.na(Actual_Node), Actual_Node, as.character(Node))) %>%
  
  # extraction of study and country
  mutate(
    Hypothesis = substr(Network, 1, 2),
    Rest = str_remove(Network, "^H[0-2]_"),
    Study = str_extract(Rest, "Study[1-3](_[A-Z]+)?"),
    CountryRaw = str_remove(Rest, paste0(Study, "_")),
    CountryName = case_when(
      CountryRaw == "DEU" ~ "Germany",
      CountryRaw == "ESP" ~ "Spain",
      CountryRaw == "FRA" ~ "France",
      CountryRaw == "GBR" ~ "United Kingdom",
      CountryRaw == "ITA" ~ "Italy",
      CountryRaw == "POL" ~ "Poland",
      CountryRaw == "SWE" ~ "Sweden",
      CountryRaw %in% c("USA", "USA_ACTS") ~ "United States",
      TRUE ~ CountryRaw
    )
  )

# Ordered (top to bottom)
node_levels <- unique(cent_plot_data$Node)
indices <- c("CM", "CB")
cm_items <- sort(node_levels[str_starts(node_levels, "cm_")])
cb_items <- sort(node_levels[str_starts(node_levels, "cb_")])

# Factor and reverse so sumscores are at the top 
cent_plot_data$Node <- factor(cent_plot_data$Node, levels = c(indices, cm_items, cb_items))
cent_plot_data$Node <- fct_rev(cent_plot_data$Node) 

# Plot
hyp_colors <- c("H0" = "#A9A9A9", # Baseline = Neutral Grey
                "H1" = "#008080", # CM Context = Teal
                "H2" = "#702963") # CB Context = Purple

for (study_id in unique(cent_plot_data$Study)) {
  
  df_study <- cent_plot_data %>% 
    filter(Study == study_id) %>%
    arrange(desc(Node)) 
  
  n_countries <- length(unique(df_study$CountryName))
  ncol_val <- ifelse(n_countries == 1, 1, 2)
  plot_height <- max(7, ceiling(n_countries / ncol_val) * 4)
  
 p_profile <- ggplot(df_study, aes(x = Strength, y = Node)) +
    
    # Zero-line reference 
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "#E41A1C", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    
    # Group by hypo and draw the line
    geom_path(aes(group = Hypothesis, color = Hypothesis), size = 1.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    
    # Draw points 
    geom_point(aes(color = Hypothesis), size = 3.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    
    facet_wrap(~ CountryName, ncol = ncol_val, scales = "fixed") + 
    
    # UPDATE 2: Clean, short labels
    scale_color_manual(values = hyp_colors, labels = c("H0", "H1", "H2")) +
    
    labs(
      x = "Standardized Strength Centrality (Z-Score)",
      y = NULL, 
      color = "Network Context:"
    ) +
    
    theme_masterpiece() +
    theme(
      # UPDATE 1: Legend relocated to the bottom
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, color = "#1A1A1A"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, color = "#1A1A1A"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "#E5E5E5", size = 0.5, linetype = "dotted")
    )
  
  ggsave(here("Output", "Robustness", paste0("Centrality_Profiles_", study_id, ".png")),
         plot = p_profile, width = 10, height = plot_height, dpi = 600, type = "cairo")
}

```
